text: Hi guys, Dr. Charles Chabert here, urologist and director of the Prostate Clinic.
The following video highlights for you some intraoperative images of how a da Vinci robotic platform is controlled.
The term robot is in effect a misnomer as there is no automatic movement of the instruments unless it is preceded by control from the surgeon.
The surgeon operates the da Vinci robotic platform by immersing their head in a 3D console, controlling the tips of the instruments using pincer-like grips.
Movements at the fingertip level are then translated through to movements at the end of the instruments to facilitate delicate dissection.
For more information please subscribe to our YouTube channel and if you like this please give us a thumbs up.
